topic.what: Large-scale multi-vector phishing prompt dataset and evaluation framework (GuardPhish) for assessing open-source LLM robustness
topic.how: Constructs 70,015 prompts across 4 attack vectors from real-world campaigns; evaluates 8 open-source LLMs under fully offline conditions; demonstrates enforcement gap between intent classification and generative refusal; trains transformer classifiers as pre-generation filters
topic.why_matters: Open-source LLMs deployed offline lack cloud-based guardrails and exhibit critical enforcement gaps—models achieving 96% phishing detection rates nevertheless generate deployable phishing content at up to 98.5% ASR

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of curated phishing prompt samples
characteristics.vs_baseline: First multi-vector phishing dataset for open-source LLM evaluation

characteristics.what_itasures: Fleiss kappa for multi-model labeling reliability
characteristics.context: 5-model ensemble (LLaMA 3.1 8B, Gemma 2 27B, Qwen 2.5 7B, Phi-3 14B, Mistral Small 24B)
Models correctly identify phishing intent but still generate malicious content

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of outputs constituting deployable phishing artifacts
characteristics.context: Demonstrates enforcement gap across all 8 open-source models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fine-tuned transformer classifier performance on phishing detection
characteristics.context: Pre-generation filters achieving near expert accuracy

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of distinct phishing attack vectors represented
characteristics.vs_baseline: First dataset covering web, email, SMS, and voice

good_for: phishing prompt evaluation for open-source LLMs
benchmarking enforcement gap across LLM architectures
requires: curation for sensitive prompt handling (restricted access)

novelty: First large-scale multi-vector phishing prompt dataset (70K prompts, 4 vectors, 42 scenarios)
First systematic empirical demonstration of detection-generation enforcement gap in open-source LLMs
First evaluation framework for fully offline inference conditions without cloud guardrails
First pre-generation filter pipeline using lightweight classifiers (98.27% accuracy) deployable without modifying base models
open_problems: Enforcement gap between intent detection and generative refusal persists across all models and vectors
Voice phishing consistently yields highest exploitability (98.5% ASR) due to conversational framing alignment
SMS phishing hardest to exploit at scale due to brevity constraints and structural sparsity
Standardized guardrail assessment protocols needed combining classification and generative refusal evaluation
Layered adaptive refusal mechanisms becoming prerequisite for security-sensitive deployments
Rapidly expanding open-source model ecosystem warrants systematic pre-deployment safety auditing
